_comment: Lowest-priority safety net: only used when nothing else posted this run, so the account still posts roughly once/hour. All entries are generic engagement prompts or evergreen, non-time-sensitive facts -- deliberately nothing with a specific price/date/number, since that would need to trace to a real live data source. Picked without repeats until the whole list is exhausted, then reshuffled.
posts: BTC, ETH, or SOL -- which one are you watching closest this week? 👀
Bulls or bears today? 🐂🐻

Zoom out. Most daily noise doesn't matter a year from now.
The market can stay irrational longer than you can stay solvent.
Not your keys, not your coins.
Time in the market usually beats timing the market.
Plans are nothing without risk management.
Every cycle feels different until it rhymes with the last one.
Volatility is the price of admission for long-term returns.
The best trades often feel the most uncomfortable going in.
Cash is a position too.
Discipline beats conviction when the trade goes against you.
Markets climb a wall of worry and slide down a slope of hope.
The trend is your friend, until it bends.
Risk comes from not knowing what you're doing.
Patience is a full-time position.
Bull markets are born on pessimism, they don't die of old age.
A good exit plan matters more than a good entry.
The crowd is usually right in the middle, and wrong at the extremes.
Don't confuse a bull market with skill.
Position sizing matters more than being right.
Liquidity disappears exactly when you need it most.
The hardest part of investing is doing nothing.
Narratives move markets faster than fundamentals do.
Every dip looks obvious in hindsight.
Conviction without a plan is just hope.
Small, consistent decisions compound more than big bold ones.

Bitcoin's total supply is capped at 21 million coins.
Ethereum moved from proof-of-work to proof-of-stake in 2022.
The S&P 500 tracks 500 of the largest publicly traded US companies.
A stock split doesn't change a company's market cap, just the share count.
ETFs let you trade a basket of assets like a single stock.
Bitcoin halvings cut the block reward in half roughly every four years.
Market cap is price multiplied by circulating supply, not total value locked.
Dollar-cost averaging means investing a fixed amount at regular intervals.
A bear market is typically defined as a 20%+ drop from recent highs.
Gas fees on Ethereum fluctuate with network congestion.
The Fear & Greed Index blends volatility, momentum, and sentiment into one score.
Stablecoins aim to hold a 1:1 peg with a fiat currency like the US dollar.
A blockchain is just a distributed, append-only ledger.
Index funds aim to match a benchmark, not beat it.
Options give the right, not the obligation, to buy or sell at a set price.
Liquidity refers to how easily an asset can be bought or sold without moving its price.
A whale, in crypto slang, is a wallet large enough to move markets on its own.
Yield curves are widely watched as a signal on economic expectations.
Diversification doesn't eliminate risk, it just spreads it around.
Most Layer 2 networks exist to make blockchains cheaper and faster to use.
Circulating supply can differ a lot from total or max supply.
A 'flippening' refers to one crypto asset overtaking another by market cap.
Short selling means profiting when an asset's price falls, not rises.
Rebalancing a portfolio just means resetting it back to your target allocation.
Compounding rewards patience more than it rewards precision.
